mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent within a single month, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending proxies and driving immediate positive equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding retail sales indicate synchronized consumer demand recovery, which lifts corporate revenue guidance and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon when both series trend upward simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the median return of non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, supply chain bottlenecks force inventory drawdowns that eventually trigger restocking cycles, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as corporate margins recover.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following consecutive INDPRO declines are not statistically higher than baseline returns (p > 0.05), or the signal produces negative returns in over 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in real GDP proxies and retail sales reflects underlying economic momentum that directly supports equity earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days without requiring threshold crossings.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, borrowing costs suppress corporate investment and consumer credit demand, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in average hourly earnings signal accelerating wage inflation, which initially boosts equity returns through higher nominal GDP expectations before the policy response materializes.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls and PAYEMS rises month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the median return of non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.0 percent, unexpected surges in housing starts signal resilient consumer demand that overrides rate hike fears, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets price in nominal growth.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent and HOUST rises month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where CPI exceeds 3.0 percent and housing starts rise.
